channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: These 7 Super Bowl Commercials Bombed—and the Internet Didn’t Hold Back
item.description: From Svedka’s AI-generated Fembot to Ritz’s salty island, here are the brands that fumbled on advertising’s biggest stage.

item.title: How Trump’s position on U.S. home prices risks a ‘generational war’ in the midterms
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to keep home prices high , bypassing calls to ramp up construction so people can afford what has been a ticket to the mid...

item.title: AI Super Bowl commercials: All the spots from Anthropic, OpenAI, Amazon, Google, Meta, and others
item.description: Some people love watching the Super Bowl for the game. Others love it for the commercials. If you’re in the latter group, you’ll probably have noticed...

item.title: Hims & Hers is scrapping its plan for a knockoff Wegovy weight-loss pill. Here’s why
item.description: Telehealth company Hims & Hers dropped its plan to offer a knockoff version of the weight-loss pill Wegovy on Saturday — two days after it announced t...

item.title: Startups Are Quietly Cutting Equity Payouts—Except at AI Companies
item.description: The ownership perk that motivates may people to enter the tech field is getting less lucrative.

item.title: AI notetakers are creating HR nightmares
item.description: The meeting ends. The AI doesn’t.

item.title: Free Starbucks coffee today: How to get your cup after hometown Seattle Seahawks won the Super Bowl
item.description: If you were up late celebrating the Seattle Seahawks’ win at the Super Bowl last night, you may need help from caffeine to get you through your busy M...

item.title: The 10 Best Super Bowl Commercials That Won the Internet
item.description: These ads were a hit with audiences during the biggest marketing moment of the year.

item.title: Weight Watchers CEO: what the GLP-1 Super Bowl ads are missing
item.description: Those ads don’t show what happens next. How do people sustain results over time? And what does a healthy, livable lifestyle look like?

item.title: 3 Ways to Prevent Division in a Multigenerational Workplace
item.description: This is not just a people strategy. It is a business imperative.

item.title: Meet the 10 Black Fortune 500 CEOs leading companies with over $412 billion in combined revenues
item.description: Black CEOs represent just 2% of the largest U.S. corporations.

item.title: ‘Take the vaccine’ urges Dr. Oz as the U.S. measles outbreak rages on
item.description: A leading U.S. health official on Sunday urged people to get inoculated against the measles at a time of outbreaks across several states and as the Un...

item.title: OpenAI vs. Anthropic Super Bowl ad clash signals we’ve entered AI’s trash talk era—and the race to own AI agents is only getting hotter
item.description: From Super Bowl ads to social media sniping, the rivalry has spilled beyond benchmarks into a fight over brand, trust, and who AI agent marketshare

item.title: The Super Bowl reveals a dangerous gap in corporate strategy
item.description: Every Super Bowl tends to follow a predictable pattern: massive marketing investment followed by temporary commercial impact.

item.title: After the Super Bowl, Seahawks Coach Mike Macdonald Kept Repeating 2 Words. It’s a Lesson in How to Win
item.description: Macdonald says “stacking wins” is a core philosophy of the Seahawks. Here’s what it means, and how you can use it in your workplace.
